channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire appears to hold as Lebanese stream south to their homes
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — A ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah that began Wednesday appeared to be holding, as residents in cars heaped with belongings streamed back toward southern Lebanon despite warnings from Israeli and Lebanese troops that they stay away from certain
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ranked-choice advocates forced to regroup after election losses
item.description: Proponents of ranked-choice voting are recalibrating after Americans across the country broadly rejected measures intended to implement the system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: What dynamic pricing means for Black Friday shopping
item.description: If it feels like prices are on a roller coaster ride during the holidays, you’re not imagining things.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barroso, Fox 5

item.title: Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade seen as attractive terrorist target: report
item.description: There's no specific threat to the biggest Thanksgiving celebration in the country, but authorities from the NYPD up to the federal government are on alert.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan ends lockdown of its capital after Imran Khan supporters are dispersed by police
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Authorities reopened roads linking Pakistan's capital with the rest of the country, ending a four-day lockdown, on Wednesday after using tear gas and firing into the air to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who marched to Islamabad to demand his rele
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's threat to impose tariffs could raise prices for consumers, colliding with promise for relief
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — If Donald Trump makes good on his threat to slap 25% tariffs on everything imported from Mexico and Canada, the price increases that could follow will collide with his campaign promise to give American families a break from inflation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart's DEI rollback signals a profound shift in the wake of Trump's election victory
item.description: Walmart’s sweeping rollback of its diversity policies is the strongest indication yet of a profound shift taking hold at U.S. companies that are re-evaluating the legal and political risks associated with bold programs to bolster historically underrepresented groups in business.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson And Cathy Bussewitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court agrees to end Trump’s classified documents case
item.description: The 11th Circuit Court of Appeals on Tuesday granted a request from special counsel Jack Smith to drop President-elect Trump’s Florida documents case, ending the battle to charge him over retaining records with classified markings after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police officer rescues sleeping man after rolling off bridge
item.description: Authorities with the Oklahoma Police Department say, a rescue incident led to one man being saved in just the nick of time.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thanksgiving Travel Latest: TSA braces for ‘busiest ever’ holiday
item.description: The Thanksgiving travel rush is expected to be bigger than ever this year. AAA predicts that nearly 80 million people in the U.S. will venture at least 50 miles (80 kilometers) from home between Tuesday and next Monday. Another round of wintry weather could complicate travel, though.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon hiker, 61, vanishes with her 2 dogs
item.description: A search is underway for Susan Lane-Fournier, a 61-year-old hiker who vanished near Welches, Oregon, late last week, the local sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare and Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs for millions of obese Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of Americans with obesity would be eligible to have popular weight-loss drugs like Wegovy or Zepbound covered by Medicare or Medicaid under a new rule the Biden administration proposed Tuesday morning. The costly proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dog treats distributed to Florida recalled for potential salmonella
item.description: A chicken-based dog treat sold online and in stores across 19 states has been recalled over the potential risk of salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel approves proposed ceasefire with Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday recommended his Cabinet approve a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rudy Giuliani draws rebuke for a courtroom outburst accusing judge in assets case of being unfair
item.description: In an angry outburst in a New York courtroom, Rudy Giuliani has accused a judge of making wrong assumptions about him after ordering him to turn over most of his assets to two election poll workers who won a libel case against him.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drought is causing saltwater to creep up the Delaware River. Here's what's being done about it
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Salty oceanwater is creeping up the Delaware River, the source for much of the drinking water for Philadelphia and millions of others, brought on by drought conditions and sea level rise, and prompting officials to tap reservoirs to push the unpotable tide back downstream.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lauren Boebert Cameo page disappears amid House ethics concerns
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.) briefly offered videos on Cameo, a website on which public figures sell personalized videos to fans, before the page went offline amid questions about whether it would have violated House ethics rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Tank explodes, catches fire near GM facility in Michigan
item.description: A tank containing byproducts from oil drilling operations exploded and caught fire Tuesday near the General Motors Proving Ground in southeastern Michigan, damaging two nearby homes but causing no injuries, a fire chief said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Popular thyroid drug linked to bone loss: Study
item.description: Levothyroxine, commercially known as Synthroid among other names, is commonly used to treat an underactive thyroid.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Repealing no-fault divorce has so far stalled across the US. Some worry that'll change under Trump
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Married couples across the U.S. have had access to no-fault divorce for more than 50 years, an option many call crucial to supporting domestic abuse victims and key to preventing already crowded family courts from drowning in complicated divorce proceedings.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-police activist blew $75K in charity funds for luxury vacation rentals, other personal expenses: AG
item.description: The founder of a DC-based police reform nonprofit allegedly misused charitable donations "to fund lavish vacations and shopping sprees," prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare, Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs like like Wegovy or Ozempic
item.description: A bipartisan coalition of congressional members have lobbied for the drugs to be covered by Medicare, saying it could save the government from spending billions of dollars on treating chronic ailments that stem from obesity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Walmart becomes largest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion policies
item.description: Walmart, the world’s largest retailer, is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion policies, joining a growing list of major corporations that have done the same after coming under attack by conservative activists.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Philadelphia teenagers charged in series of 'unprovoked' attacks within an hour
item.description: Six teenagers were charged in a series of what investigators described as "unprovoked" attacks around Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, that happened within an hour.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto industry's shift toward EVs is expected to go on despite Trump threat to kill tax credits
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — If President-elect Donald Trump makes good on his threat to kill federal tax credits for electric vehicle purchases, it’s likely that fewer buyers will choose EVs.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER AP Auto Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge in LA delays until January decision on resentencing Menendez brothers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge on Monday delayed until January his decision on whether to resentence Erik and Lyle Menendez for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion 35 years ago, squashing their family’s hope the brothers would be released and home for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Walmart ordered to pay former truck driver $35M after jury finds fraud claims false
item.description: A jury in California found Walmart defamed a truck driver with false claims of workers' compensation fraud and will have to pay the former worker more than $34 million in damages.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Dictionary.com Word of the Year is very mindful, very cutesy
item.description: Dictionary.com was very mindful and very cutesy when choosing its 2024 Word of the Year. This year’s title went to viral sensation "demure," a word popularized by TikToker Jools Lebron. “In making the annual selection, Dictionary.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Jack Smith files to drop Jan. 6 charges against Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith has filed a motion to drop all four felony charges against President-elect Donald Trump in connection with his effort to overturn his 2020 presidential election in the lead-up to the deadly Jan. 6 attack on the U.S Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly and Ken Dilanian, NBC News, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First right whales of season gorge on critical food off Massachusetts, giving hope for a strong year
item.description: Scientists who study a critically endangered species of whale that lives off New England said encouraging early signs suggest the animals could have a strong season for feeding and breeding.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father of Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles found dead near LAX
item.description: Ryan Kobayashi, father of a 31-year-old Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles nearly two weeks ago, was found dead near Los Angeles International Airport early Sunday morning, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, KRON4

item.title: GOP senator: Americans don’t care about FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty (R-Tenn.), who was rumored to be on Trump’s shortlist to serve as Treasury secretary, said most Americans “don’t care” whether Trump uses the FBI to vet his nominees, something fellow Republican senators have demanded.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Here's how AI can help you out in the kitchen
item.description: Cooking can be challenging or relaxing, but either way, AI is having an impact in the kitchen. Recently, I tried out a new tool called BakeBot AI. It's sort of like ChatGPT but customized for the kitchen.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: What's behind the doors of this Missouri cave? A former roller rink, concert stage and more
item.description: Adventure awaits at every turn of one captivating cave in suburban St. Louis, now modernized into a quirky and nostalgic getaway with a storied legacy.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, ABC 4

item.title: Traveling for Thanksgiving? Winter storms possible across US during holiday week
item.description: Forecasters throughout the U.S. issued warnings that another round of winter weather could complicate travel leading up to the Thanksgiving holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chiefs' Patrick Mahomes fined over $14K for 'violent gesture' at Bills game
item.description: The fine comes after the star quarterback celebrated a touchdown pass with a hand gesture that appeared to imitate him holding a gun.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WFLA, NBC
